notes: A central concept in Japanese social harmony. The phrase 'not to trouble others' (人に迷惑をかけない) is a foundational social rule in Japan. It covers everything from light noise to serious physical or financial burden.

grammar_points.usage: Use をかける when an agent actively causes the trouble. Use がかかる (intransitive) when the trouble falls upon someone due to a situation or external cause.

archetypes: 迷惑がられないようにする
grammar_points.usage: The verb suffix 〜がる indicates that someone else is showing signs of an emotion. Combined with the passive, it describes the state of being regarded as a nuisance by others.
meanings: to be treated/viewed as a nuisance by (Person)

archetypes: ご迷惑をおかけしました
ご迷惑をおかけして申し訳ありません
grammar_points.usage: A highly productive business and formal phrase. It uses the お〜する humble formula even though 迷惑 is a noun, typically paired with the causative-leaning verb かける to express deep regret for the burden caused.

phrases.ja: 近所迷惑[きんじょめいわく]
ありがた迷惑[めいわく]
迷惑[めいわく]をかける
ご迷惑[めいわく]をおかけしました
phrases.en: I'm sorry for the trouble I caused

phrases.ja: 迷惑[めいわく]極[きわ]まりない
迷惑[めいわく]そうな顔[かお]
phrases.en: an annoyed look; a troubled expression

examples.ja: 夜遅[よるおそ]くにピアノを弾[ひ]くのは、近所迷惑[きんじょめいわく]になる。
examples.en: Playing the piano late at night is a nuisance to the neighbors.

examples.ja: 電車[でんしゃ]の中[なか]で大声[おおごえ]で話[はな]す人[ひと]には本当[ほんとう]に迷惑[めいわく]している。
examples.en: I am truly annoyed by people who talk loudly on the train.

examples.ja: これ以上[いじょう]、家族[かぞく]に迷惑[めいわく]をかけたくない。
examples.en: I don't want to cause any more trouble for my family.
